title: Mayo Clinic Identifies First Aptamers That Selectively Tag Senescent Cells
description: Mayo Clinic researchers in Aging Cell screened 100+ trillion synthetic DNA sequences and identified rare aptamers that bind selectively to surface proteins (notably a fibronectin variant) on senescent cells. The technology promises a cheaper, more adaptable alternative to antibodies and — critically — could eventually carry senolytic payloads directly to zombie cells, opening a precision-targeting frontier the field has lacked.

title: Mount Sinai Reverses Aging in Blood Stem Cells via Lysosome Reset
description: Mount Sinai team in Cell Stem Cell used a vacuolar ATPase inhibitor to calm hyperactive lysosomes in aged hematopoietic stem cells, restoring youthful regenerative function with an 8x increase in blood-forming ability ex vivo. The result identifies lysosomal hyperactivity as a central driver of stem cell aging and points toward immediate translational applications for age-related blood disorders, stem cell transplantation, and gene therapy conditioning.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Wraps After Four Days as Field's Premier Capital-Formation Event
description: Vitalist Bay 2026 closed Sunday at Berkeley's Lighthaven after four days, 100+ speakers, 150+ workshops and 18 onsite health tests across Biotech, Investors, Replacement, Longevity Science, AI x Bio, and Biostasis tracks. The festival is now widely viewed as the field's annual private-market price discovery event, with capital formation an explicit operating goal alongside its scientific programming.

title: Life Biosciences ER-100 Partial Reprogramming Phase 1 Approaches Dosing
description: Life Biosciences' AAV-delivered three-factor (OCT4/SOX2/KLF4) gene therapy moves toward dosing in up to 12 glaucoma patients followed by 6 NAION patients, with an antibiotic-controlled genetic switch and a 5+ year safety window. Monkey safety data showed no tumors or adverse signals — the c-Myc omission appears to have held, validating the cautious clinical path into the first human partial reprogramming trial.

title: Senescent Cell Targeting Pipeline Bifurcates: Detection vs Elimination
description: The Mayo Clinic aptamer breakthrough complements Imperial College's GPX4 chloroacetamides and Rubedo's RLS-1496 by giving the field its first precision targeting modality — a way to identify senescent cells before deciding whether to flag, image, or kill them. Combined with Mount Sinai's lysosome-reset rejuvenation work, the senescence playbook is now diversifying into clear detection, rejuvenation, and elimination lanes.

title: Grad Student's Idea Sparks Mayo Clinic Aptamer-Based Senescence Detection
description: Aging Cell paper describes Mayo Clinic team's identification of rare aptamers that selectively bind senescent cell surface proteins — including a fibronectin variant — using a 100+ trillion sequence screen.

title: Mount Sinai Reverses HSC Aging by Targeting Lysosomal Hyperactivity
description: Cell Stem Cell publication shows vacuolar ATPase inhibitor restored lysosomal function in aged HSCs, increasing blood-forming ability 8-fold ex vivo with improvements in mitochondria, epigenetics, and inflammation.

title: This Method to Reverse Cellular Aging Is About to Be Tested in Humans
description: Life Biosciences' partial reprogramming gene therapy will be tested in glaucoma and NAION patients with antibiotic-controlled three-factor delivery, 5+ year safety window, and David Sinclair as co-founder.

title: Life Biosciences Lands $80M Series D for ER-100 Phase 1
description: $80M Series D funds Phase 1 of partial reprogramming cell reset therapy targeting glaucoma and optic neuropathies; alongside GeneRare EUR 20M Series A and Noom 503A pharmacy acquisition.

title: Avai Bio Introduces Klotho-Based Anti-Aging Therapy at Premier Forum
description: Avai Bio (OTCQB: AVAI) details cell-based alpha-Klotho longevity therapy via Klothonova JV with Austrianova; data to be presented at Second Annual Klotho Conference in September.

title: Vitalist Bay 2026 Returns to Berkeley May 14-17
description: Vitalism Foundation's pop-up longevity festival at Lighthaven Berkeley with 100+ speakers, 150+ workshops, 18 onsite activities and health tests across six themes.

title: Dietary Changes in Older People Can Improve Biological Age
description: Aging Cell paper shows 65-75 year olds who reduced fat or animal-based protein for four weeks registered measurable reductions in biological age via biomarker profile.

title: Insilico Announces Industry's First Longevity Board
description: Andrew Adams (Lilly) chairs new Longevity Board with Nobel laureate Michael Levitt; mandate covers life models, biomarkers of aging, dual-purpose targets, and clinical validation of healthspan effects.

title: Human Longevity Launches $599 Clinical-Grade Whole Genome Sequencing
description: Human Longevity, Inc. unveiled a $599 clinical-grade whole genome report with AI-driven prevention insights, honoring founder J. Craig Venter's legacy.

title: Large Rapamycin Clinical Trial Launches at UT Health San Antonio
description: Multi-phase clinical study with ~84 older adults will determine optimal rapamycin dosage for healthy aging via a six-month treatment, six-month follow-up RCT against placebo and intermittent dosing.

title: Annovis Advances Buntanetap in Phase 3 Alzheimer's and Parkinson's Trials
description: Annovis's buntanetap reduces amyloid beta, tau, and alpha-synuclein translation in Phase 3 Alzheimer's and Parkinson's; sildenafil combos boost cerebral blood flow via NO-cGMP pathway.

title: BioAge Labs R&D Day: BGE-102 Phase 1 Shows Big hsCRP Drops
description: BioAge presented Phase 1 results for BGE-102 with 24-hour coverage at 60 mg once-daily dosing and profound hsCRP reductions in obese subjects, setting up cardiovascular and DME POC studies.

title: Creatine Shows Synergy With Exercise in Older Adults
description: Spanish researchers in Experimental Gerontology showed creatine combined with power training improved BDNF, antioxidant defense, inflammation, and lower-limb function in 103 older adults over 16 weeks.

title: New Senolytic Strategy Targets GPX4 in Ageing Cells
description: Gil and Tate groups identify chloroacetamide senolytics that selectively kill senescent cells by inhibiting GPX4 and triggering ferroptosis.

title: Rubedo Reports Positive Phase 1 Results for RLS-1496
description: First-in-class topical GPX4 modulator was well tolerated with dose-related target engagement, senescent-cell reduction, lower inflammatory cytokines, and ~20% reduction in epidermal thickness.

title: Insilico Medicine and Eli Lilly Sign $2.75B AI Drug Discovery Pact
description: Lilly grants Insilico $115M upfront with up to $2.75B in milestones plus tiered royalties for exclusive worldwide license to a pipeline of oral preclinical assets.

title: Noom Acquires 503A Compounding Pharmacy for GLP-1/Peptide Distribution
description: Noom buys 503A pharmacy to dispense GLP-1s, sermorelin, and NAD+ alongside behavioral coaching — vertical integration validates GLP-1 as first consumer-tier longevity drug.

title: GeneRare Raises EUR 20M Series A for Microbial Genome Geroprotector Discovery
description: EUR 20M Series A scales microbial genome scanning for natural products; libraries from plants, fungi and marine sources targeting rapamycin analogs and other geroprotectors.

title: Longevity Biotech Stocks Rally Narrative Forms Around $27T Healthcare Transformation
description: Industry narrative builds around cell-restoration therapies as catalysts for $27 trillion global healthcare transformation; specific stock percentages not disclosed.

title: Q1 2026 Longevity Biotech Investment Hits $3.74B Across 49 Deals
description: DLT dataset projects 2026 capital flows of $8-9B (55-60% YoY growth), with median deal size of $21.8M signaling a maturing pipeline.

title: Inexion: $554M Q4 Longevity Funding Yet RWE Data Gap Persists
description: Q4 2025 longevity biotech funding hit $554M (900% above Q3), but no major claims-first data platform reaches the cash-pay longevity cohort.

title: NYT Magazine: Cellular Rejuvenation Has the Potential to Reverse Aging
description: Long-form feature on partial reprogramming, Altos Labs, and the first safety trial of rejuvenation therapy in humans targeting glaucoma.

title: Junyue Cao on How the Body Ages, Cell by Cell
description: Rockefeller researcher discusses ultra-high-throughput single-cell technologies applied to mapping cellular hallmarks of aging across tissues.

title: Seragon SRN-901 Extends Median Lifespan 33% in Aged Mice
description: SRN-901 multipathway treatment of 18-month-old mice produced a 33% increase in median remaining lifespan plus reductions in frailty and tumor incidence.

title: Sleep Apnea, Intermittent Hypoxia, and p16-Mediated Senescence
description: Preprint links sleep apnea-induced intermittent hypoxia to p16Ink4a senescence in vascular endothelium, suggesting senescence component to CPAP-resistant cardiovascular risk.
